rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL structural rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks at valid locations without modifying other elements
Use visible identifiers from the current context when available, but do not assume their existence
Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes to maintain type-level abstraction
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks at the appropriate scope level (component type or implementation) based on the contract's purpose
Preserve the existing AADL structure by only adding annex blocks and not modifying other elements
Use visible identifiers when available, but do not assume their existence or create new ones without validation
Avoid using implementation-specific details in component type contracts to maintain type-level abstraction
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, equations, constants, and assignments
Place input assumptions and output guarantees in the appropriate scope based on the contract's purpose
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes to maintain type-level abstraction
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as explicit ports or constants
Do not modify the existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
